6

 ADC1(2)

•

•

SOURCE: 
– PFD

LOGIC:

– This is not a Fault.   The ADC SOURCE Knob is NOT set to NORM

7

 A/F ARM

•

•

SOURCE
– PEC

LOGIC

– Indicates Autofeather system is armed. The A/F ARM message is presented 

in reverse video during the first 2 seconds of display.
– Autofeather is armed under the following conditions:

• Power levers beyond 60° PLA
• Torques above 50%
• Condition levers at MAX/1020
• Engines running
• Previous Autofeather test was successful.

• MAINTENANCE

– Redo Autofeather test       if A/F ARM does not appear. 
– NOTE: Do not cycle FADEC or PEC circuit breakers, a POWERPLANT 

message will be set.

8

 A/F SELECT

•

•

SOURCE
– PEC

LOGIC

– Indicates Autofeather system is Selected On for Takeoff 
– Autofeather is Selected under the following conditions:

• Condition levers at MAX/1020
• Engines running
• Previous Autofeather test was successful.

• MAINTENANCE

– Redo Autofeather test       if A/F SELECT does not appear. 
– NOTE: Do not cycle FADEC or PEC circuit breakers, a POWERPLANT 

message will be set.

9

 A/F TEST ABORT

•

•

SOURCE: 
– PEC

LOGIC:

– PEC1 or 2 senses: 

• Loss of the autofeather select signal during the test. 
• CLA not at START/FEATHER or FUEL OFF during the test. 
• PLA not at DISC during the test
• Loss of the autofeather permissive signal from the opposite engine.
• Loss of the opposite powerplant torque signal indicating that the opposite 

powerplant is producing useful power.

• PEC changing lanes during test.

• MAINTENANCE:

– Redo Autofeather test 
– NOTE: Do not cycle FADEC or PEC circuit breakers, a POWERPLANT message will 

be set.  

•

FIM TASK 61-41-00-810-809 (L)  and 810 (R)

10

 A/F TEST FAILED

•

•

SOURCE: 
– PEC

LOGIC:

– PEC1 or 2 senses: 

•

•

•

Internal PEC Automatic Underspeed Protection Circuit (AUPC) ARM MONITOR is 
not armed when the arming conditions are set.  
Internal PEC AUPC ARM MONITOR armed when the arming conditions are not 
set.
Internal PEC AUPC THRESHOLD MONITOR is inactive (no AUPC drive signal) 
with NQA/NQB more than 83% (845 rpm).  
Internal PEC AUPC TEST STATUS is test not passed.  
Internal PEC AUPC TEST ABORT is test aborted.

•
•
• AUPC is activated (possibly by flight deck mishandling). 

• MAINTENANCE

– Redo AutoFeather Test. 
– NOTE: Do not cycle FADEC or PEC circuit breakers, a POWERPLANT message will 

be set.  

•

FIM  TASK 61-41-00-810-807 (L) and 808 (R)

11

 A/F TEST IN PROG

•

•

SOURCE: 
– PEC

LOGIC:

– Indicates Autofeather system test is in progress 
– Autofeather is tested under the following conditions:

• CONDITION levers – FUEL OFF or START/FEATHER
• POWER levers – DISC
• AUTOFEATHER switch - Press. 

12

 A/F TEST PASSED

•

•

SOURCE: 
– PEC

LOGIC:

– Indicates Autofeather system test was successful 
– Autofeather is tested under the following conditions:

• CONDITION levers – FUEL OFF or START/FEATHER
• POWER levers – DISC
• AUTOFEATHER switch - Press. 

13

 AFCS CONTROLLER INOP

•

•

SOURCE: 
– FGM

LOGIC:

– A FGM senses a Flight Guidance Control Panel (FGCP) malfunction or that 

one of the FGCP switches are stuck. 

• MAINTENANCE

– Circuit Breaker Reset

• FG CP 1 Avionics G7
• FG CP 2 Avionics G6

– Then

•
•

IOM/IOP FGM 1 Avionics F7
IOM/IOP FGM 2 Avionics F6

•

FIM Task TASK 22-11-00-810-821

14

 AFCS FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 
– PFD

LOGIC:

– A PFD does not receive valid data from the two flight guidance modules 

(FGMs).

• MAINTENANCE

– Circuit Breakers Reset

•
•

IOM/IOP FGM 1 Avionics F7
IOM/IOP FGM 2 Avionics F6

– Aircraft Power Reset

•

FIM TASK 22-11-00-810-845 (PFD1), -868 (PFD2)

15

 ALT

•

•

SOURCE
– IOPs

LOGIC

– The PFD shows the ALT mismatch flag when the Flight Data Processing 
System (FDPS) senses and Air Data Unit 1 (ADU1) and ADU2 altitude 
difference that is more than 60 feet at Sea Level up to 180 feet at FL250
– The PFD flight mode annunciator (FMA) can also show an ALT MISMATCH 

message.

– NOTE: The FMAs will show only the most important mismatch message 

and this is the primary mismatch indication.

• MAINTENANCE

– Circuit Breaker Reset

• ADU 1 Avionics  A7
• ADU 2 Avionics  A6

•

FIM TASK 34-11-00-810-872

16

 ALT FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 

– Display Unit

LOGIC:

– The Display Unit does not receive valid altitude data from the ADU

• MAINTENANCE

– Circuit Breaker Reset

• ADU 1 Avionics  A7
• ADU 2 Avionics  A6

•

FIM TASK 34-11-00-810-852 (PFD1), -853 (PFD2), 

17

 ALT MISMATCH

•

•

SOURCE: 
– IOP

LOGIC:

– ADU1 and ADU2 altitude difference that is more than 60 feet at Sea Level to 

180 feet at FL250.

• MAINTENANCE

– Circuit Breaker Reset

• ADU 1 Avionics  A7
• ADU 2 Avionics  A6

•

FIM TASK 34-11-00-810-850

18

 ALT OFF

•

•

SOURCE

– FGMs

LOGIC

– This is not a fault
– Yellow message, flashing for 5 seconds, then steady.
– When the Pitch Thumbwheel is used in Altitude Capture or Altitude Hold 

mode

19

 AP

•

•

SOURCE
– FGM

LOGIC

– AP displayed in green when Autopilot is engaged and functioning,

20

 AP DISENGAGED

•

•

SOURCE

– FGMs

LOGIC

– Displayed following a Yaw Damper and Autopilot disengagement, either automatic or 

manual (AP selected OFF)

– For automatic disengagement, message flashes until reset, then steady for 5 seconds. 
– Steady for 5 seconds when manual disengagement
– The Autopilot disengages when one of the following conditions is met:

•
•
•
•
•
•
•

•
•
•
•
•
•
•
•
•
•
•

AHRS 1 or AHRS 2 is failed
AHRS 1 and AHRS 2 disagree
ADC 1 or ADC 2 is failed
ADC 1 and ADC 2 disagree
The aircraft is not airborne
The crew press the AP or YD pushbutton on the FGCP. (Manual disengagement)
The crew operates manual pitch trim, setting the Manual Pitch Trim AP Disconnect discrete, except if TCS is 
active. (Manual  disengagement)
The crew manually override the AP using the control column or wheel
The crew press an AP Disengage Switch. (Manual disengagement)
The TCS function fails
A Stall Warning AP Disconnect discrete from SPM#1 or SPM#2 is set
A GA switch is selected. (Manual disengagement)
An AP actuator failure is detected
The Yaw Damper disengages 
A Push-button on FGCP is stuck
Internal AFCS monitoring is failed
A Power interrupt greater than the transparency time occurs
The aircraft attitude is outside the domain limits (+/- 50 degrees roll angle and +/- 25 degrees pitch angle).

• MAINTENANCE

– Reset by push and hold A/P DIS       switches located on either control wheel

21

 AP/YD DISENGAGED

•

•

SOURCE

– FGMs

LOGIC

– Displayed following a Yaw Damper and Autopilot disengagement, either automatic or 

manual (AP selected OFF)

– For automatic disengagement, message flashes until reset, then steady for 5 seconds. 
– Steady for 5 seconds when manual disengagement
– The Autopilot disengages when one of the following conditions is met:

•
•
•
•
•
•
•

•
•
•
•
•
•
•
•
•
•
•

AHRS 1 or AHRS 2 is failed
AHRS 1 and AHRS 2 disagree
ADC 1 or ADC 2 is failed
ADC 1 and ADC 2 disagree
The aircraft is not airborne
The crew press the AP or YD pushbutton on the FGCP. (Manual disengagement)
The crew operates manual pitch trim, setting the Manual Pitch Trim AP Disconnect discrete, except if TCS is 
active. (Manual  disengagement)
The crew manually override the AP using the control column or wheel
The crew press an AP Disengage Switch. (Manual disengagement)
The TCS function fails
A Stall Warning AP Disconnect discrete from SPM#1 or SPM#2 is set
A GA switch is selected. (Manual disengagement)
An AP actuator failure is detected
A Yaw Damper actuator failure is detected
A Push-button on FGCP is stuck
Internal AFCS monitoring is failed
A Power interrupt greater than the transparency time occurs
The aircraft attitude is outside the domain limits (+/- 50 degrees roll angle and +/- 25 degrees pitch angle).

• MAINTENANCE

– Reset by push and hold A/P DIS       switches located on either control wheel

22

 AP FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 
– FGMs

LOGIC:

– Internal FGM, APAU-P, APAU-R, or pitch (P) or roll (R) inhibit malfunction 

that prevents AP operation.

– NOTE:  If the AP or YD was engaged, an AP DISENGAGED message is 

shown until cancelled before this message is shown. 

– NOTE:  The FMA in the PFD can also show the YD NOT CENTERED 

message.

• MAINTENANCE

– Press and hold red AP DIS switch       on pilot or co-pilot control column 

•

FIM TASK 22-11-00-810-808

23

 AP INHIBIT

•

•

SOURCE: 
– FGMs

LOGIC:

– Displayed in white when Autopilot engagement is attempted and an AFCS external 

monitor inhibits engagement due to:

• AHRS monitoring.
• ADC monitoring.
• The aircraft is not airborne.
• The aircraft attitude exceeds the AP engagement limits.
• An AP Disengage Switch is currently pressed.
• A GA (Go Around) switch is selected.
• TCS (Touch Control Steering) is active. 
• The Manual Pitch Trim AP Disconnect discrete is set. 
• A Stall Warning AP Disconnect discrete from SPM#1 or SPM#2 is set.  
• The AP disengagement warnings are active.

• MAINTENANCE

– Press and hold red AP DIS switch       on Pilot / Copilot control column 
– Then reset breakers

•
•

IOM/IOP FGM 1 Avionics F7
IOM/IOP FGM 2 Avionics F6

•

FIM TASK 22-11-00-810-842

24

 AP PITCH TRIM FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 
– FGM

LOGIC:

– A Failure of the AP Pitch Trim
– No pitch trim motion when commanded by the Autopilot.
– Pitch trim motion in the opposite sense of the Autopilot command.
– FGM Monitor pitch actuator (APAU-P) torque values disagree.
– There is a malfunction of the left and right channels of the FCECU

• MAINTENANCE

– Circuit Breaker Reset

• FCS ECU 1 LH DC M3
• FCS ECU 2 RH DC L6

•

FIM TASK 22-10-00-810-802

25

 AP/YD FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 
– FGM

LOGIC:

– Internal FGM or YDAU malfunction that prevents YD operation.
– NOTE:  If the AP/YD was engaged, an AP/YD DISENGAGED message is shown until 

cancelled before this message is shown.

– NOTE:  If the YD was engaged, an YD DISENGAGED message is shown until 

cancelled before this message is shown

• MAINTENANCE

– Press and hold red AP DIS switch on pilot or co-pilot control column 
– Then Reset Breakers

• YD AU (ACTUATOR UNIT) Avionics E3

– Then

•
•

IOM/IOP FGM 1 Avionics F7
IOM/IOP FGM 2 Avionics F6

•

FIM TASK 22-11-00-810-805

26

 ATT1(2)

•

•

SOURCE: 
– PFD

LOGIC:

– This is not a Fault.   The ATT/HDG SOURCE Knob is NOT set to NORM

27

 ATT FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 

– Display Unit (PFD)

LOGIC:

– The Display Unit does not receive valid pitch or roll data from the selected 

AHRU. 

• MAINTENANCE

– Circuit Breaker Reset

• AHRS 1 – Avionics A4
• AHRS 1 – Avionics A9
• AHRS 2 – Avionics A10
• AHRS 2 – Avionics A3

•

FIM TASK 34-21-00-810-851 (PFD1), 852(PFD2)

28

 AUTO TRIM FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 
– FGM

LOGIC:

– A failure of the Flap Auto Pitch Trim function
– FGM does not receive valid data from the two Flap Position Indication Unit (FPIU) 

channels.

– No pitch trim motion when commanded by the FGM.
– Pitch trim motion in the opposite sense of the FGM command.
– There is a malfunction of the left and right channels of the FCECU

• MAINTENANCE

– Circuit Breaker Reset

• FLAP POSN IND R - RH DC F7
• FLAP POSN IND L - LH DC M7

– Cycle Aircraft Power. 

•

FIM TASK 22-11-00-810-828

29

 BALANCE

•

•

SOURCE: 

– Fuel Quantity Computer (FQC)

LOGIC:

– Yellow flashing continuously and yellow fuel quantity figures if 600lb 

imbalance

• MAINTENANCE

– Transfer Fuel on FUEL CONTROL PANEL

30

 BLEED

•

•

SOURCE

– FADEC

LOGIC

– BLEED air switches 1 and/or 2 are selected on
– NOTE: Bleed selection of NORM or MAX with NTOP engine rating (PLA @ 

Rating Detent and CLA @ 1020) will change the BLEED indication to 
YELLOW.

– NOTE: Bleed selection of NORM or MAX with MTOP engine rating will 

change the rating to MCP (Max Continues Power).

31

 CAT2 FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 
– FGMs

LOGIC:

–

This message is shown when Dual FD approach mode is cancelled while the  
Decision Height (DH) is set less than 200 feet and more than 50 feet. The Dual FD 
submode is cancelled by the following:
• A failure of one or both FGMs.
• A failure of a parameter from either AHRS that is used by the Localizer Track or 

GlideslopeTrack modes.

• Changing the EFIS ATT/HDG Source Selection to the '1' OR '2' position.
• A failure of a parameter from either ADC that is used by the Localizer Track or 

Glideslope Track modes.

• Changing the EFIS ADC Source Selection to the '1' OR '2' position.
• An ILS data failure for 5 seconds or a mismatch between the ILS data.
• Changing either left or right Nav Source Selection.
• Changing the frequency of either left or right VOR/LOC Receiver.
• Automatic or manual cancellation of Localizer Track or Glideslope Track mode.
•
• A mismatch between the FD commands.
• Activating TCS. 

Invalid Radio Altitude data.

32

 CHECK ED

•

•

SOURCE: 
– MFD

LOGIC:

– An MFD senses a primary ED indication discrepancy for one or more critical 

parameters:· TRQ, NH,  NH red limit, NP,  ITT.

– NOTE:  If MFD 1 or MFD 2 is set to show ED indications, it will be 

monitored by the other MFD.

• MAINTENANCE

– Turn MFDs and ED all OFF then back ON one at a time. 

• PFD1/2 and MFD1/2 on EFIS Control Panels
• ED on ESID Control Panel

– Cycle Aircraft Power.

•

FIM  31-61-01-810-807

33

 CHECK NAV SOURCE

•

•

SOURCE
– FGMs

LOGIC

– Engagement of FD NAV mode or VNAV mode attempted and wrong NAV 

source selected 

• White message, steady for 5 seconds

– FD armed or active NAV mode dropped off following a NAV source change, 

or a VOR/LOC NAV frequency change. 

• A Yellow message, flashing for 5 seconds, then steady until failure is 

cleared by changing the NAV source selection or by selecting another 
FD mode. 

34

 CHECK PFD1

•

•

SOURCE: 

– MFD1 or PFD2

LOGIC:

– The MFD1 or PFD2 senses a difference in PFD1 indication for one or more 

of these parameters:· Altitude, Airspeed, Pitch, Roll, Heading. 

• MAINTENANCE

– Turn all PFDs and MFDs (EFIS Control Panel) Off then back ON one at a 

time. 

– Cycle Aircraft Power.

•

FIM  31-61-01-810-804

35

 CHECK PFD2

•

•

SOURCE: 

– MFD2 or PFD1

LOGIC:

– The MFD2 or PFD1 senses a difference in PFD2 indication for one or more 

of these parameters:· Altitude, Airspeed, Pitch, Roll, Heading. 

• MAINTENANCE

– Turn all PFDs and MFDs (EFIS Control Panel) Off then back ON one at a 

time. 

– Cycle Aircraft Power.

•

FIM  31-61-01-810-805

36

 CUE

•

•

SOURCE: 

– Stall Protection Modules

LOGIC:

– A SPM is defective 
– Difference between AOA1 and AOA 2 
– AOA1(2) is defective or not heated 
– FPIU left and right flap position inputs are defective.
– ADU1(2) CAS, TAS or pitot pressure input is defective 
– AHRS1(2) input is defective 
– FADEC1(2) (A and B channels) engine torque input is defective 
– NOTE:  The CUE flag is out of view when WOW and KIAS is < 60 knots.

• Maintenance

– Circuit Breaker Reset

• SPM 1 Avionics F2
• SPM 2 Avionics F5

– Cycle Aircraft Power

•

FIM 27-33-00-810-830  (PFD1),  -831 (PFD2)

37

 DU BAD CONF

•

•

SOURCE: 

– PFD, MFD, or ED

LOGIC:

– PFD1(2), MFD1(2) or ED senses that it does not receive valid aircraft 

configuration data from the two aircraft configuration modules, or that the 
data is different. 

– NOTE:  The Display Unit BAD CONF message can come into view only 

after a Display Unit POST. 

– NOTE:  The AVIONICS caution light will also come on.

• MAINTENANCE

– Turn ALL Displays OFF then back ON one at a time.

• PFD1/2 and MFD1/2 on EFIS Control Panels
• ED on ESID Control Panel

– Cycle Aircraft Power

•

FIM 31-61-01-810-806

38

 DUAL FD

•

•

SOURCE
– FGM

LOGIC

– This is not a fault
– Dual Flight Director Mode is active
– The Dual FD mode is a submode of the ILS Approach mode which indicates 

to the flight crew that the AFCS is in a configuration valid for Cat II ILS 
approaches. In the Dual FD submode,both FGMs independently display FD 
commands on the PFDs, with FGM#1 providing the FD commands for the 
left PFD, and FGM#2 providing FD commands for the right PFD. In the Dual 
FD mode, FGM#1 uses Localizer and Glideslope deviations from the left 
VOR/LOC Receiver, air data from ADC#1, attitude and Vertical Speed data 
from AHRS#1, and left course data from the FGCP. FGM#2 uses Localizer 
and Glideslope deviations from the right VOR/ LOC Receiver, air data from 
ADC#2, attitude and Vertical Speed data from AHRS#2, and right course 
data from the FGCP.

39

 DUAL OFF

•

•

SOURCE: 
– FGM

LOGIC:

– The FGM senses a Dual FD mode malfunction during ILS approach that is not Cat II. 

This message is shown when Dual FD approach mode is cancelled. 

– The Dual FD submode is cancelled by:
• A failure of one or both FGMs.
• A failure of a parameter from either AHRS that is used by the Localizer Track or Glideslope 

Track modes.

• Changing the EFIS ATT/HDG Source Selection to the '1' OR '2' position.
• A failure of a parameter from either ADC that is used by the Localizer Track or Glideslope Track 

modes.

• Changing the EFIS ADC Source Selection to the '1' OR '2' position.
• An ILS data failure for 5 seconds or a mismatch between the ILS data.
• Changing either left or right Nav Source Selection.
• Changing the frequency of either left or right VOR/LOC Receiver.
• Automatic or manual cancellation of Localizer Track or Glideslope Track mode.
•
• A mismatch between the FD commands.
• Activating TCS.

Invalid Radio Altitude data.

– NOTE:  The Cat II ILS mode operates when the DH is set less than 200 feet and more 

than 50 feet.

•

FIM 22-11-00-810-836

40

 ED MON FAIL

•

•

SOURCE: 
– ED

LOGIC:

– The set ED senses that its parameters are not monitored by a different 

Display Unit for more than 30 seconds. 

– The AVIONICS caution light will also come on.
– NOTE:  The ED is monitored by MFD1 and MFD2. 
– NOTE:  Each Display Unit has a feed back output bus that supplies the 

status of its monitoring function to the set ED. 

• MAINTENANCE

– Turn ALL Displays OFF then back ON one at a time.

• PFD1/2 and MFD1/2 on EFIS Control Panels
• ED on ESID Control Panel

– Cycle Aircraft Power

•

FIM TASK 31-61-01-810-808

41

 •

•

FADEC1/DU FAIL

SOURCE: 

– Display Unit showing Engine Data (ED or MFD)

LOGIC:

– The FADEC1 output channel used for indication has malfunctioned for more 
than 10 seconds while the aircraft is less than 50 KIAS and the engines are 
not operating.

• MAINTENANCE

– Make Sure #1 FADEC Circuit Breakers are all in 

• ENG 1 FADEC A - LH DC J4
• ENG 1 FADEC B - RH DC J4

– Turn ED (MFD) OFF then back ON
– Then Cycle Aircraft Power. 
– NOTE: Do not cycle FADEC or PEC circuit breakers, a POWERPLANT 

message will be set.  

•

FIM 77-31-00-810-814

42

 •

•

FADEC2/DU FAIL

SOURCE: 

– Display Unit showing Engine Data (ED or MFD)

LOGIC:

– The FADEC 2 output channel used for indication has malfunctioned for 
more than 10 seconds while the aircraft is less than 50 KIAS and the 
engines are not operating.

• MAINTENANCE

– Make Sure #2 FADEC Circuit Breakers are all in 

• ENG 2 FADEC A -LH DC K4
• ENG 2 FADEC B -RH DC K4

– Turn ED (MFD) OFF then back ON
– Then Cycle Aircraft Power. 
– NOTE: Do not cycle FADEC or PEC circuit breakers, a POWERPLANT 

message will be set.  

•

FIM 77-31-00-810-815

43

 •

•

FADECS/DU FAIL

SOURCE: 

– Display Unit showing Engine Data (ED or MFD)

LOGIC:

– Both FADEC’s output channels used for indication have malfunctioned for 
more than 10 seconds while the aircraft is less than 50 KIAS and the 
engines are not operating.

• MAINTENANCE

– Make Sure Both FADEC Circuit Breakers are all in 

• ENG 1 FADEC A - LH DC J4
• ENG 1 FADEC B - RH DC J4
• ENG 2 FADEC A - LH DC K4
• ENG 2 FADEC B - RH DC K4
– Turn ED (MFD) OFF then back ON
• ED on ESID Control Panel

– Then Cycle Aircraft Power. 
– NOTE: Do not cycle FADEC or PEC circuit breakers, a POWERPLANT 

message will be set.  

•

 INFO: Autofeather Test

•

AMM Task TASK 61-20-00-710-801 Autofeather Test

– NOTE: This check can be completed with the two engines running or with the two 

engines stopped.

– Make sure that the two condition lever angles (CLA's) are at START AND FEATHER 

(engine running) or at FUEL OFF (engine stopped).

– Make sure the two power lever angles (PLA's) are at DISC.
– Push the AUTOFEATHER switch light to SELECT on, on the propeller control panel:
• Make sure that the A/F SELECT and the A/F TEST IN PROGRESS messages 

show on the engine display. 

– Make sure the A/F TEST PASSED message shows on the engine display after the test 

sequence is completed.

•

•
•
•

IF the test does not pass, interrogate/record Powerplant fault codes.

– Note: Code 494 indicates last A/F test did not pass. 

Cycle Aircraft power and repeat Autofeather Test. 
IF the test still does not pass, interrogate faults again and troubleshoot
IF the test passes, clear fault codes.

– PEC

LOGIC

– Indicates Autofeather system is armed. The A/F ARM message is presented 

in reverse video during the first 2 seconds of display.

• Power levers beyond 60° PLA
• Torques above 50%
• Condition levers at MAX/1020
• Engines running
• Previous Autofeather test was successful.

sections.title: • MAINTENANCE

• Loss of the opposite powerplant torque signal indicating that the opposite 

powerplant is producing useful power.

– Displayed in white when Autopilot engagement is attempted and an AFCS external 

monitor inhibits engagement due to:

• AHRS monitoring.

• The aircraft is not airborne.
• The aircraft attitude exceeds the AP engagement limits.
• An AP Disengage Switch is currently pressed.
• A GA (Go Around) switch is selected.
• TCS (Touch Control Steering) is active. 
• The Manual Pitch Trim AP Disconnect discrete is set. 
• A Stall Warning AP Disconnect discrete from SPM#1 or SPM#2 is set.  
• The AP disengagement warnings are active.
